pi_items.source: Discovered during live testing: without admission record, patient chart shows 'Access Denied'. Fixed in setup_task.sh lines 23-26. Confirmed by other tasks: record_vitals/setup_task.sh line 40, add_ongoing_concerns/setup_task.sh line 43 (evidence: 04_access_denied_before_fix.png, db_admission.txt)

FDA prescribing information (https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/drugsatfda_docs/label/2019/021995s046lbl.pdf): 'recommended dosage is 100 mg orally once daily'; Drugs.com (https://www.drugs.com/dosage/sitagliptin.html); Mayo Clinic (https://www.mayoclinic.org/drugs-supplements/sitagliptin-oral-route/description/drg-20069730)
Medscape (https://reference.medscape.com/drug/januvia-zituvio-sitagliptin-342730); export_result.sh searches for both 'Sitagliptin' and 'Januvia' in drug fields
FDA label: 'Sitagliptin Tablets are a dipeptidyl peptidase-4 (DPP-4) inhibitor indicated as an adjunct to diet and exercise to improve glycemic control in adults with type 2 diabetes mellitus'

pi_items.verified_value: 52-83% higher hypoglycemia risk than other sulfonylureas; ADA recommends avoiding in older adults
pi_items.source: NCBI StatPearls (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK545313/); Mayo Clinic (https://www.mayoclinic.org/drugs-supplements/glyburide-oral-route/side-effects/drg-20072094); Cleveland Clinic (https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/drugs/18948-glyburide-tablets)

pi_items.verified_value: Above ADA target of <7%, indicates poor glycemic control requiring medication adjustment
pi_items.source: ADA (https://diabetes.org/about-diabetes/a1c): target <7%; PMC (https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC8179883/): target 7-7.7% reduces complications; NCBI StatPearls (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK549816/)

pi_items.verified_value: Significantly elevated; normal fasting is 4.0-5.4 mmol/L, diabetes threshold >=7.0 mmol/L, 9.2 equals ~166 mg/dL
pi_items.source: Cleveland Clinic (https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/diagnostics/21952-fasting-blood-sugar); Mayo Clinic (https://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/diabetes/diagnosis-treatment/drc-20371451)

pi_items.verified_value: Stage 2 hypertension (systolic >=140 mmHg); consistent with hypertensive diabetic patient
pi_items.source: American Heart Association (https://www.heart.org/en/health-topics/high-blood-pressure/understanding-blood-pressure-readings): Stage 2 is >=140/>=90

pi_items.verified_value: Skin rash and hives are the most common sulfonamide allergy manifestation (63% of cases per PMC study)
pi_items.source: PMC (https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6789825/): 'majority reported rash or hives (n=59; 63%)'; Cleveland Clinic (https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/diseases/25131-sulfa-allergy); DermNet NZ (https://dermnetnz.org/topics/sulfa-drugs-and-the-skin)

pi_items.verified_value: OSCAR uses type codes: BP (blood pressure), WT (weight), HT (height), HR/PULSE (heart rate), HbA1c/A1C (glycated hemoglobin), FBS (fasting blood sugar)

pi_items.verified_value: OSCAR McMaster - Open Source Clinical Application and Resource, developed by McMaster University since 2001, Java/JSP on Tomcat with MySQL/MariaDB, GPL v2
pi_items.source: Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/OSCAR_McMaster); McMaster University (https://fammed.mcmaster.ca/oscar-emr/); OSCAR Galaxy (https://oscargalaxy.org/about/brief-overview-of-oscar/); live screenshot footer confirms 'OSCAR McMaster is copyrighted and trade-marked by McMaster University' (evidence: 05_login_page_after_fix.png)

privileged_info_summary: Patient Fatima Al-Hassan (DOB: August 9, 1978, demographic_no=7, sex=F, HIN=7890123456 ON) is a synthetic patient in the OSCAR McMaster EMR environment. The task requires completing an annual diabetic care review with 7 clinical actions across 6 EMR modules. Initial state after setup: 4 active medications (Metformin 500mg BID drugid=5, Glyburide 5mg OD drugid=6, Ramipril 10mg OD drugid=7, Atorvastatin 20mg OD drugid=8), 1 allergy (Penicillin/Anaphylaxis/Severe allergyid=4), 0 measurements, 0 encounter notes, 0 ticklers. The agent must: (1) record vitals BP 142/88 mmHg (Stage 2 hypertension per AHA), HR 78 bpm, Weight 81.2 kg, Height 163 cm using OSCAR measurement types BP/HR/WT/HT; (2) record HbA1c 8.1% (above ADA target <7%) and Fasting Blood Glucose 9.2 mmol/L (~166 mg/dL, well above 7.0 threshold) using types A1C/FBS; (3) discontinue Glyburide 5mg by setting archived=1 (hypoglycemia risk 52-83% higher than other sulfonylureas per NCBI StatPearls); (4) prescribe Sitagliptin/Januvia 100mg PO once daily, qty 90, 3 repeats (standard DPP-4 inhibitor dosage per FDA prescribing information); (5) add Sulfonamides allergy with reaction 'Skin rash and hives' (most common manifestation at 63% per PMC study) and Moderate severity; (6) write encounter note containing keywords diabetic, HbA1c, Glyburide, Sitagliptin, sulfonamide; (7) create tickler due approximately 90 days from today with diabetes follow-up message. OSCAR EMR accessed at http://localhost:8080/oscar/login.jsp with credentials oscardoc/oscar/PIN:1117. Critical setup detail: an admission record (program_id=1, admission_status='current') must exist for the patient to access their encounter chart - without it OSCAR returns 'Access Denied'. The export_result.sh queries MySQL via docker exec oscar-db and produces JSON with 35+ verification fields. All data is synthetic (seeded via SQL).
